label: PagerDuty Incident
description: Trigger / acknowledge / resolve a PagerDuty incident via Events API v2. Supports dedupKey, severity, custom_details, and client/client_url fields.

settings.name: routingKey
settings.label: Routing key
settings.description: PagerDuty integration routing key. Falls back to PAGERDUTY_ROUTING_KEY env when unset.

settings.name: dedupKey
settings.label: Dedup key
settings.description: Stable key for the incident — required for acknowledge/resolve to target an existing incident.

settings.name: customDetails
settings.label: Custom details
settings.description: Free-form JSON object passed through as payload.custom_details on trigger events.

settings.name: client
settings.label: Client name
settings.description: Name of the client posting the event (e.g. 'OpenAgentic Workflows').

settings.name: clientUrl
settings.label: Client URL
settings.description: URL the client name links to in PagerDuty (deep-link back to the workflow run).

ai.shortDescription: Trigger, acknowledge, or resolve a PagerDuty incident via Events API v2.
ai.whenToUse: When you need to page on-call about a workflow failure, ack an incident from a known-fixed signal, or auto-resolve when health restores.
ai.examplePrompt: page on-call when a deployment fails, including the build URL as the client_url
ai.promptHints: Set dedupKey to a stable identifier so retries collapse into the same incident. Use action=acknowledge or resolve to update an existing incident.

outputAssertions.name: status_202
outputAssertions.errorMessage: PagerDuty Events API returned non-202 status (the API replies 202 Accepted on success).
